# prq

> Developer attention CLI — see what needs you across GitHub and Linear. One command.

prq consolidates PRs, issues, and tickets from GitHub and Linear into a single prioritized queue. Instead of checking GitHub notifications, Linear, and Slack separately, developers run one command to see what needs their attention — categorized by action type.

## Install

npm install -g prq-cli

Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be authenticated.

## Categories

- Needs Re-review: new commits pushed after your review
- Requested: you're a requested reviewer
- Stale: no activity for N days
- Your PRs Waiting: waiting on someone else
- In Progress: PRs you've marked as started

## Key Features

- Interactive TUI with keyboard navigation
- Generic filtering (--filter label:priority, !author:bot, type:pr)
- Default filters in config for persistent preferences
- Custom actions via .prqrc.json (integrates with Claude Code, Codex, gh CLI)
- JSON output for scripting and agent workflows
- Nudge stale PRs without double-pinging
- Claude Code skill (/prq)
- Zero config — works the moment your tools are authenticated

## Links

- Website: https://prq.sh
- GitHub: https://github.com/caiopizzol/prq
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/prq-cli
